ops.title: Claude Code plugin cache re-pulls only on a version change, not a new git SHA ‚Äî content-only commits at the same version go stale and show \"not cached\"; EXCEPTION: version-less plugins (e.g. captain-hook) key on the SHA, so a content commit DOES bust t
ops.body:
The active copy of a Claude Code plugin loads from `~/.claude/plugins/cache/<marketplace>/<plugin>/<version>/` ‚Äî a plain (non-git) copy keyed by the `version` in `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`. The marketplace itself (e.g. `skills` ‚Üí `~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/skills`) is a github clone that `autoUpdate`s, but **the cache does NOT re-pull when only the git SHA changes at the same version.**

So a content-only edit to a skill that's committed+pushed without bumping `version` never reaches the running plugin: `/plugins` shows "not cached ‚Ä¶ Run /plugins to refresh", and `claude plugin update <plugin>@<marketplace>` no-ops with "already at the latest version (X)". Confirmed live: the writing-docs slop-cop edit (and an earlier great-docs note at `0e4b6bc`) stayed stale at 0.2.0 until bumped.

**Fix:** bump the plugin's `version` (e.g. 0.2.0 ‚Üí 0.2.1), push, then `claude plugin marketplace update <mkt>` + `claude plugin update <plugin>@<mkt> --scope <scope>`. The plugin is installed per-scope (writing-docs is `--scope project` for `/Users/yasyf/Code/cc-notes`, not user) ‚Äî match the scope or the update reports "not installed." Changes need a Claude Code restart to apply to the running session. A local-only alternative (no commit) is uninstall+reinstall, but it leaves the version stale so the same bug recurs.

**Exception ‚Äî version-less plugins key on the git SHA.** A plugin whose marketplace entry has NO `version` field caches under the git SHA, not a semver ‚Äî so `installPath` ends in the SHA and a plain content commit DOES bust the cache: just `claude plugin marketplace update <mkt>` then `claude plugin update <plugin>@<mkt> --scope <scope>`. Confirmed live 2026-06-18 on captain-hook. **captain-hook LEFT this class on 2026-07-13**: its plugin.json carries `version` (9.9.0+, added so consumer plugins can pin a dependency floor per [[capt-hook-attach-only-pack-contract]]) ‚Äî every captain-hook plugin-content change now REQUIRES a version bump like any other plugin (its cache dirs look like `9.9.0-<sha>`). The exception still describes any remaining version-less plugin.

Source of truth is always the repo (`./plugins/<name>`), never the cache or the marketplace clone ‚Äî the clone gets clobbered by its auto `git pull`. Related: [[always-push-commits]], [[captain-hook-consume-packs-not-vendor]].
